Course Title: Poor Data Quality in Construction AI
Category: Infrastructure & Construction: Construction, Engineering, Building Materials, Real Estate Development
Who is this for: Project managers, construction managers, site engineers, building inspectors, real estate developers, construction supervisors, engineering technicians, data analysts in construction
Team or Dept this is for: Construction, Engineering, Project Management, Real Estate Development, Operations, Quality Assurance
Description: How incomplete or outdated data impacts decisions.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with construction or engineering workflows and a general curiosity about how data is used in decision-making are all you need to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the most common sources of poor data quality in construction projects and explain how incomplete or outdated information can lead to costly errors in AI-driven decision making. You will also apply practical strategies to recognise data quality issues early, helping you to support more reliable and informed outcomes across construction, engineering, and real estate development workflows.
